title: BCIT Auditory Cueing
description: The BCIT Auditory Cueing dataset comprises EEG, eye-tracking, and vehicle performance data collected during prolonged simulated driving tasks with auditory cues and lateral perturbations. Subjects performed two 45-minute driving conditions: one with randomly presented auditory tones and another with tones predictive of vehicle perturbations. The study was designed to identify driver fatigue biomarkers through analysis of EEG data in relation to objective performance measures and subjective fatigue scales.
methods_description: Subjects performed simulated driving tasks using a Real Time Technologies driving simulator (900 Hz video refresh rate, 100 Hz vehicle data logging). EEG was recorded using BioSemi 64-channel systems with 4 eye and 2 mastoid channels at 2048 Hz sampling rate. Eye tracking was performed using Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250. The Auditory Cueing protocol consisted of two 45-minute driving conditions with lateral vehicle perturbations: Condition A with randomly presented auditory tones and Condition B with tones predictive of perturbation onset. Dependent measures included reaction times, vehicle control metrics (steering angle, lane position, heading error), and subjective fatigue scales (TIFS, KSS, VAS-F).
